headline: Bumble shares surge as investors swipe right on AI-powered reboot
summary: STORY: Shares of dating app Bumble skyrocketed nearly 50% Thursday morning after the company posted upbeat fourth-quarter revenue and unveiled an AI‑driven overhaul of its apps.The stock's move higher comes after years of losses and battered investor confidence, with shares losing half their value last year as growth in the online dating market slowed amid stiff competition.Bumble CEO Whitney Wolfe Herd is betting that a revamped product could attract younger users who complain of swiping fatigue.To that end, the company is preparing to launch Bumble 2.0 that uses AI to enhance quick photo swipes.Herd also said that Bumble could experiment with a "no‑swipe" experience in some markets.Dating apps like Hinge, owned by Match Group, are also rolling out AI‑powered tools aimed at winning back younger users.Analysts, however, say they are looking for "meaningful innovation" in an industry that has seen little change since the swipe‑based design became standard...and they remain slightly cautious about how much Bumble's redesign will change its fortunes long-term.

headline: Bumble Beats Estimates Because Thirst Is Macro-Proof
summary: Whitney Wolfe Herd came back to clean up a mess, and while she was at it, Match Group got kicked out of the S&P 500

headline: Social media giants urged to tighten child safety after UK rejects blanket ban for teens
summary: Social media firms need to use better age verification technologies to keep children off their platforms, the U.K.'s Information Commissioner's Office said.

headline: Bumble shares rally on earnings beat, optimism around AI‑led app revamp
summary: March 12 - Bumble (BMBL) shares surged 20% in premarket trading on Thursday, as stronger‑than‑expected fourth‑quarter results and an AI‑focused overhaul of its ‌dating app aimed at winning back younger users reignited investor ‌optimism.
